title: Tool-Surface Entropy framing (reply to Bora Celik in Mitko Vasilev's MCP thread)
author: Carsten Lindstedt
author_headline: Industrial AI | Industry World Models · Virtual Twins · Agentic Systems · Physical AI
notes: Reply by Carsten Lindstedt to Bora Celik's reply in Mitko Vasilev's 'I deleted all my MCPs' thread. Carsten introduces the 'tool-surface entropy' framing: the real variable isn't MCP-vs-CLI but whether one controls action-space entropy. Steel-mans both sides: concedes Zechner's controlled-setup result while explaining why naive large MCP fails at scale. Notes progressive disclosure shifts the problem to retrieval, which is unreliable at scale.
